canonical_definition:
Across the provided literature, "Natural language reasoning" is predominantly characterized as a latent or inherent capability of large language models to understand, manipulate, and draw logical inferences from text to perform complex tasks. While most definitions converge on this general framing, the specific application of this reasoning varies. A common view describes it as the ability to solve problems by generating step-by-step derivations and explanatory text, particularly in domains like mathematics. Another prevalent framing defines it as the capacity to discern "complex, implicit relationships between documents" (HLM-Cite: Hybrid Language Model Workflow for Text-based Scientific Citation Prediction) or understand logical connections between sentences.

This construct is operationalized through various observed model behaviors. For example, it is discussed in the context of generating natural language solutions to problems, sometimes in contrast to "tool manipulation" (JiuZhang3.0: Efficiently Improving Mathematical Reasoning by Training Small Data Synthesis Models). Papers describe its application in tasks such as ranking scientific papers, proposing "valid causes for certain findings" (LLM-CXR: Instruction-Finetuned LLM for CXR Image Understanding and Generation), and performing contradiction detection. One paper explicitly uses a sentence entailment task on the XNLI benchmark to test this capability. While most sources treat it as a general text-based skill, a few contrast it with other modalities, noting that models can possess strong "textual reasoning" even when failing at "Visual Interpretation" (NL-Eye: Abductive NLI For Images).
papers: ModelCitizens: Representing Community Voices in Online Safety
When Big Models Train Small Ones: Label-Free Model Parity Alignment for Efficient Visual Question Answering using SmallVLMs
LVLMs are Bad at Overhearing Human Referential Communication
MMLU-ProX: A Multilingual Benchmark for Advanced Large Language Model Evaluation
LLM-CXR: Instruction-Finetuned LLM for CXR Image Understanding and Generation
MathCoder: Seamless Code Integration in LLMs for Enhanced Mathematical Reasoning
PB-LLM: Partially Binarized Large Language Models
Self-contradictory Hallucinations of Large Language Models: Evaluation, Detection and Mitigation
Understanding Catastrophic Forgetting in Language Models via Implicit Inference
Vision-by-Language for Training-Free Compositional Image Retrieval
Learning to Plan Before Answering: Self-Teaching LLMs to Learn Abstract Plans for Problem Solving
LiveCodeBench: Holistic and Contamination Free Evaluation of Large Language Models for Code
NL-Eye: Abductive NLI For Images
OmnixR: Evaluating Omni-modality Language Models on Reasoning across Modalities
Steering Large Language Models between Code Execution and Textual Reasoning
To Code or Not To Code? Exploring Impact of Code in Pre-training
HLM-Cite: Hybrid Language Model Workflow for Text-based Scientific Citation Prediction
JiuZhang3.0: Efficiently Improving Mathematical Reasoning by Training Small Data Synthesis Models
On Learning Multi-Modal Forgery Representation for Diffusion Generated Video Detection
Symbolic Regression with a Learned Concept Library

definitions.text: The ability to comprehend and analyze textual content to discern complex, implicit relationships between documents.
definitions.paper: HLM-Cite: Hybrid Language Model Workflow for Text-based Scientific Citation Prediction

definitions.text: The ability to solve problems by generating explanatory text and step-by-step derivations in human language.
definitions.paper: JiuZhang3.0: Efficiently Improving Mathematical Reasoning by Training Small Data Synthesis Models

definitions.text: The latent capability of a large language model to understand, manipulate, and draw logical inferences from natural language text.
definitions.paper: Symbolic Regression with a Learned Concept Library

definitions.text: The ability to reason over extracted textual content and produce a final answer.
definitions.paper: OmnixR: Evaluating Omni-modality Language Models on Reasoning across Modalities

definitions.text: The latent ability of a language model to understand and manipulate language in a logical or commonsensical way to perform complex tasks.
definitions.paper: PB-LLM: Partially Binarized Large Language Models

definitions.text: The inherent capability of LLMs to generate logically coherent and contextually appropriate responses based on linguistic patterns and prior knowledge, including proposing plausible causes for observed findings.
definitions.paper: LLM-CXR: Instruction-Finetuned LLM for CXR Image Understanding and Generation

characterization:
Across the provided literature, "Natural language reasoning" is predominantly characterized as a latent or inherent capability of large language models to understand, manipulate, and draw logical inferences from text to perform complex tasks. While most definitions converge on this general framing, the specific application of this reasoning varies. A common view describes it as the ability to solve problems by generating step-by-step derivations and explanatory text, particularly in domains like mathematics. Another prevalent framing defines it as the capacity to discern "complex, implicit relationships between documents" (HLM-Cite: Hybrid Language Model Workflow for Text-based Scientific Citation Prediction) or understand logical connections between sentences.

This construct is operationalized through various observed model behaviors. For example, it is discussed in the context of generating natural language solutions to problems, sometimes in contrast to "tool manipulation" (JiuZhang3.0: Efficiently Improving Mathematical Reasoning by Training Small Data Synthesis Models). Papers describe its application in tasks such as ranking scientific papers, proposing "valid causes for certain findings" (LLM-CXR: Instruction-Finetuned LLM for CXR Image Understanding and Generation), and performing contradiction detection. One paper explicitly uses a sentence entailment task on the XNLI benchmark to test this capability. While most sources treat it as a general text-based skill, a few contrast it with other modalities, noting that models can possess strong "textual reasoning" even when failing at "Visual Interpretation" (NL-Eye: Abductive NLI For Images).
